page: Google Cloud Platform c4-highmem-32-lssd is an x86_64 architecture server powered by an Intel Xeon 6985P-C processor. It features 16 physical cores and 32 vCPUs with dedicated allocation, operating at a base frequency of 2.3 GHz. The memory configuration provides 248 GB of RAM, yielding 7.75 GB per core. For local storage, the instance includes 1745 GB of NVMe SSD capacity distributed across 5 local SSDs. The CPU features a 504 MB L3 cache and supports advanced instruction sets including AVX-512 and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions for tile and INT8 operations.

In benchmark evaluations, the server demonstrates top-tier performance in single-threaded tasks, with Geekbench single-core scores and single-threaded gzip compression placing in the top 10%. Multi-threaded decompression and Passmark CPU scores rank in the top 25%, indicating strong parallel processing capabilities. Memory performance is robust, with a Passmark memory score in the top 25%, though large-block memory bandwidth read and write operations remain in the average tier. For database workloads, the system handles 62.26 million Redis SET operations per second, placing it in the top 25% tier.

This hardware profile is highly suited for memory-intensive applications, database hosting, and localized data processing that benefits from high-speed NVMe storage. The inclusion of Intel AMX instructions enables strong performance in lightweight AI workloads, as evidenced by top-tier text generation speeds for small LLM models. However, for larger models such as the 70B parameter LLM, inference speeds drop to average levels, indicating a tradeoff where heavy AI workloads may require dedicated GPU acceleration.
description: Google Cloud Platform c4-highmem-32-lssd is an x86_64 server configured with an Intel Xeon 6985P-C processor, offering 32 dedicated vCPUs across 16 physical cores at 2.3 GHz. It features 248 GB of RAM and 1745 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. The processor includes a 504 MB L3 cache and supports Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions and AVX-512. Benchmarks show top-tier single-core performance, with high-speed single-threaded gzip compression and strong multi-threaded decompression. Database capabilities are highlighted by 62.26 million Redis SET operations per second. While highly efficient for memory-intensive databases, web serving, and lightweight LLM inference, performance on larger 70B parameter models drops to average levels, indicating a tradeoff for heavy AI tasks. This server represents a balanced, high-density resource profile for enterprise databases and memory-optimized workloads.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform c4-highmem-32-lssd features 32 vCPUs, 248 GB RAM, and 1745 GB local NVMe SSD storage. Powered by Intel Xeon 6985P-C, it delivers top-tier single-core and database performance.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform c4-highmem-32-lssd is a memory-optimized server with 32 vCPUs, 248 GB RAM, and 1745 GB NVMe SSD storage for database workloads.
tagline: A memory-optimized server featuring dedicated Intel Xeon processors and high-speed local NVMe storage for demanding database and analytical workloads.
bullet_points: 32 dedicated vCPUs powered by Intel Xeon 6985P-C processor with 504 MB L3 cache.
248 GB of RAM paired with 1745 GB of high-speed local NVMe SSD storage.
Intel AMX and AVX-512 instruction support for accelerated matrix and vector operations.
Top-tier single-core performance and strong multi-threaded decompression capabilities.
Delivers 62.26 million operations per second in Redis SET benchmarks.
Optimized for memory-intensive databases, web serving, and lightweight LLM inference.
